subject: Physics
coverageNote: Form directories exist only where teaching-module source PDFs were ingested. Missing forms must resolve as known-gap, not empty objects.

lesson.title: 2: THERMAL PHYSICS
topics: Thermal Physics is the branch of Physics that studies the thermal properties of matter, including
temperature, heat, and the behaviour of systems in relation to thermal energy. This topic introduces
learners to important concepts that explain how heat affects matter and how thermal energy is
transferred and utilised in everyday life. Knowledge of Thermal Physics is important in many
activities: HOOK: You notice that when boiling water for nshima, steam rises from the pot and
spreads around the kitchen and you wonder what is happening to the particles in the
a) Using the kinetic theory of matter, explain what happens to the particles of water
b) Why do the particles move faster as the temperature increases?
resources: Learning Activity 2.2.4.1.4 Comparing efficiency of diesel and petrol engine
In this learning activity, learners will be able to compare efficiency of diesel and petrol engine.
 Natural Materials: Air for demonstrating combustion concepts, Water for demonstrating
 Artificial Materials: Charts comparing diesel and petrol engines, Videos showing engine
assessment:  Observe learner’s participation during the discussion
 Critical Thinking: Explaining particle’s behavior using the kinetic theory of matter
